title: Gloomdivision
notes: Fantastic album. Dallon's funk and disco influences are a great touch for his style in my taste. The production quality is excellent, knowing exactly when to be quiet and only have one track playing, and when to have a falling piano noise to add chaos. Thats a good word for the album as well: chaos.
highlights: Highlights were the funky bass, emotional/vulnerable lyrics, and the contrast in energy and song sound. Songs like 'WHAT LOVE?' and 'SUNNYSIDE' are great new sounds for Dallon that I thought really fit him. I look forward to hearing more of that.
lowlights: If you are looking for P!ATD's sound, this is not it. It's not Brendon Urie. It's Dallon Weekes' album, and its angsty, vulnerable, and chaotic. I think that it really suits the vibe, but sometimes the chaos can diminish the listening. I miss certain tracks because there's too much going on.
